raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year> my XXXX fianc, my XXXX American sons and I was supposed to leave to go to XXXX. At the last minute I remembered that we needed XXXX I searched on XXXX and found a false website ( not really fake but not the real government one ). The original false website was XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX It looked " good enough '' - NOW important note : This is where I think American Express is wrong. Notice on this website what they are saying " XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX is an electronic declaration which all passengers must complete when arriving in or leaving the XXXX. '' Notice those important words " All passengers MUST complete ''. If they didn't have that there, I would have only ordered XXXX for my XXXX XXXX. She is the only one that is required to have it - XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX when it comes to departing passengers- only XXXX passengers are required to have it - unfortunately I did not read this BEFORE ordering. 

So, they lied on their website. Note : this website has the same WRONG info as XXXX XXXX XXXX - I sadly didn't notice the URL. It also says " all passengers are now required to complete the XXXX XXXX before entering and leaving the country. '' So, I paid for all XXXX of us. Now, the way the website was formatted on the iphone it appeared I was only being charged {$39.00}. That is what shows up on the screen in bold in the middle- it doesn't show the full amount {$110.00} unless you scroll ALL the way down and it is shown in small print at the bottom. So, they charged me XXXX charges of {$110.00} and XXXX charge of {$88.00}. No idea where the {$88.00} came from. I didn't notice the charge until it showed up on my credit bill since I have XXXX click pay and I didn't see the totals. 

I contacted American Express XXXX times about this -- trying to dispute and dispute the disputes. I even attached pictures of the website showing their lie and the XXXX government website showing only my fianc had to have the XXXX visa. They didn't care. The big problem is that the customer service representative told me the best way to enter this problem is to say you didn't get the service- but I told her that is not true, that is not what my problem is, but she said it is the best way. 

So, this false ( again not really fake ) website both XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX said all XXXX of us had to have the visa - only XXXX did. 
PLUS they formatted the phone display to hide the total bill until the end.

But, the big complaint is against American Express for a ) telling me to enter the complaint as not getting the service and b ) not caring about the proof I sent them about the lie on the website.

Note : On the official website the XXXX  visa is 100 % free even for last minute bookings. This FALSE website is hoping to get XXXX like me and gorge them on fees.
